question: Which of the following actions is outside the scope of practice for a massage therapist? [Variant 1]
answers: Referring a client to a physical therapist
short_explanation: Massage therapists are not permitted to diagnose medical conditions.
long_explanation: Only licensed medical professionals may diagnose conditions. Massage therapists can observe and refer clients appropriately, but should not label or interpret pathology.
clinical_application: Refer out when conditions appear outside your training. Document observations, not diagnoses.

question: A client begins disclosing personal trauma during a session. What is the ethical response? [Variant 2]
answers: Redirect to the bodywork and stay present
short_explanation: Stay within your scope and bring focus back to the massage.
long_explanation: Massage therapists must maintain professional boundaries. While compassionate listening is important, offering psychological advice or delving into trauma exceeds ethical scope and may harm the client.
clinical_application: Have a referral list for mental health providers. Practice grounding techniques if the client becomes emotional.
source_reference: AMTA Code of Ethics; FSMTB Ethics Guide

question: What must occur before initiating any massage technique or session? [Variant 3]
answers: The client must remove all clothing
A note from the client’s physician
short_explanation: Clients must give informed consent before treatment begins.
long_explanation: Informed consent means the client understands the treatment plan, has had all questions answered, and agrees voluntarily. Consent must be ongoing and can be withdrawn at any time.
clinical_application: Review any changes in treatment or client condition before each session.

question: Which situation may represent an ethical conflict of interest? [Variant 4]
answers: Accepting a tip from a client
Providing massage to a romantic partner
short_explanation: Romantic or sexual relationships with clients violate ethical guidelines.
long_explanation: Engaging in dual relationships where romantic or emotional boundaries are blurred can compromise objectivity, safety, and trust in the therapeutic relationship.
clinical_application: Refer out if emotional entanglements arise or cannot be avoided.

question: Under HIPAA and professional ethics, client records must be: [Variant 5]
answers: Shared with other therapists for peer review
Accessible to the client's family without permission
Kept secure and confidential at all times
short_explanation: Client information must remain private and protected.
long_explanation: Massage therapists must maintain client confidentiality and only share health information with written consent. Records must be stored securely to meet legal and ethical standards.
clinical_application: Have a lockable storage system and restrict access to client files.
source_reference: HIPAA Compliance for Massage Professionals; FSMTB Ethics Guide
